book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
On the Words, the Only-Begotten Son of God, Begotten of the Father Very God Before All Ages, by Whom All Things Were Made.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: On the Words, the Only-Begotten Son of God, Begotten of the Father Very God Before All Ages, by Whom All Things Were Made.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in times past unto the Fathers by the Prophets, hath in these last days spoken unto us by His Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That we have hope in Jesus Christ has been sufficiently shewn, according to our ability, in what we delivered to you yesterday.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we must not simply believe in Christ Jesus nor receive Him as one of the many who are improperly called Christs.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For they were figurative Christs, but He is the true Christ; not having risen by advancement from among men to the Priesthood, but ever having the dignity of the Priesthood from the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And for this cause the Faith guarding us beforehand lest we should suppose Him to be one of the ordinary Christs, adds to the profession of the Faith, that we believe In One Lord Jesus Christ, the Only-Begotten Son of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again on hearing of a “Son,” think not of an adopted son but a Son by nature, an Only-begotten Son, having no brother.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this is the reason why He is called “Only-begotten,” because in the dignity of the Godhead, and His generation from the Father, He has no brother.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we call Him the Son of God, not of ourselves, but because the Father Himself named Christ His Son: and a true name is that which is set by fathers upon their children.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ erewhile became Man, but by the many He was unknown.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wishing, therefore, to teach that which was not known, He called together His disciples, and asked them, Whom do men say that I, the Son of Man, am? —not from vain-glory, but wishing to shew them the truth, lest dwelling with God, the Only-begotten of God, they should think lightly of Him as if He were some mere man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when they answered that some said Elias, and some Jeremias, He said to them, They may be excused for not knowing, but ye, My Apostles, who in My name cleanse lepers, and cast out devils, and raise the dead, ought not to be ignorant of Him, through whom ye do these wondrous works.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when they all became silent (for the matter was too high for man to learn), Peter, the foremost of the Apostles and chief herald of the Church, neither aided by cunning invention, nor persuaded by human reasoning, but enlightened in his mind from the Father, says to Him, Thou art the Christ, not only so, but the Son of the living God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And there follows a blessing upon his speech (for in truth it was above man), and as a seal upon what he had said, that it was the Father who had revealed it to him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Saviour says, Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona, for flesh and blood hath not revealed it to thee, but My Father which is in heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He therefore who acknowledges our Lord Jesus Christ the Son of God, partakes of this blessedness; but he who denies the Son of God is a poor and miserable man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, I say, on hearing of a Son, understand it not merely in an improper sense, but as a Son in truth, a Son by nature, without beginning; not as having come out of bondage into a higher state of adoption, but a Son eternally begotten by an inscrutable and incomprehensible generation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And in like manner on hearing of the First-born, think not that this is after the manner of men; for the first-born among men have other brothers also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And it is somewhere written, Israel is My son, My first-born.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But Israel is, as Reuben was, a first-born son rejected: for Reuben went up to his father’s couch; and Israel cast his Father’s Son out of the vineyard, and crucified Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To others also the Scripture says, Ye are the sons of the Lord your God: and in another place, I have said, Ye are gods, and ye are all sons of the Most High.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I have said, not, “I have begotten.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They, when God so said, received the sonship, which before they had not: but He was not begotten to be other than He was before; but was begotten from the beginning Son of the Father, being above all beginning and all ages, Son of the Father, in all things like to Him who begat Him, eternal of a Father eternal, Life of Life begotten, and Light of Light, and Truth of Truth, and Wisdom of the Wise, and King of King, and God of God, and Power of Power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If then thou hear the Gospel saying, The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, the Son of Abraham, understand “according to the flesh.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He is the Son of David at the end of the ages, but the Son of God Before All Ages, without beginning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The one, which before He had not, He received; but the other, which He hath, He hath eternally as begotten of the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Two fathers He hath: one, David, according to the flesh, and one, God, His Father in a Divine manner.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As the Son of David, He is subject to time, and to handling, and to genealogical descent: but as Son according to the Godhead, He is subject neither to time nor to place, nor to genealogical descent: for His generation who shall declare?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God is a Spirit; He who is a Spirit hath spiritually begotten, as being incorporeal, an inscrutable and incomprehensible generation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Son Himself says of the Father, The Lord said unto Me, Thou art My Son, to-day have I begotten Thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now this to-day is not recent, but eternal: a timeless to-day, before all ages.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: From the womb, before the morning star, have I begotten Thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Believe thou therefore on Jesus Christ, Son of the living God, and a Son Only-Begotten, according to the Gospel which says, For God so loved the world, that He gave His Only-begotten Son, that whosoever believeth on Him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again,He that believeth on the Son is not judged, but hath passed out of death into life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But he that believeth not the Son shall not see life, but the wrath of God abideth on him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And John testified concerning Him, saying, And we beheld His glory, glory as of the only-begotten from the father,—full of grace and truth: at whom the devils trembled and said, Ah! what have we to do with Thee, Jesus, Thou Son of the living God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He is then the Son of God by nature and not by adoption, begotten of the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And he that loveth Him that begat, loveth Him also that is begotten of Him; but he that despiseth Him that is begotten casts back the insult upon Him who begat.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And whenever thou hear of God begetting, sink not down in thought to bodily things, nor think of a corruptible generation, lest thou be guilty of impiety.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God is a Spirit, His generation is spiritual: for bodies beget bodies, and for the generation of bodies time needs must intervene; but time intervenes not in the generation of the Son from the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And in our case what is begotten is begotten imperfect: but the Son of God was begotten perfect; for what He is now, that is He also from the beginning, begotten without beginning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We are begotten so as to pass from infantile ignorance to a state of reason: thy generation, O man, is imperfect, for thy growth is progressive.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But think not that it is thus in His case, nor impute infirmity to Him who hath begotten.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if that which He begot was imperfect, and acquired its perfection in time, thou art imputing infirmity to Him who hath begotten; if so be, the Father did not bestow from the beginning that which, as thou sayest, time bestowed afterwards.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Think not therefore that this generation is human, nor as Abraham begat Isaac.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in begetting Isaac, Abraham begat not what he would, but what another granted.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But in God the Father’s begetting there is neither ignorance nor intermediate deliberation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For to say that He knew not what He was begetting is the greatest impiety; and it is no less impious to say, that after deliberation in time He then became a Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God was not previously without a Son, and afterwards in time became a Father; but hath the Son eternally, having begotten Him not as men beget men, but as Himself only knoweth, who begat Him before all ages Very God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Father being Very God begat the Son like unto Himself, Very God; not as teachers beget disciples, not as Paul says to some, For in Christ Jesus I begat you through the Gospel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in this case he who was not a son by nature became a son by discipleship, but in the former case He was a Son by nature, a true Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not as ye, who are to be illuminated, are now becoming sons of God: for ye also become sons, but by adoption of grace, as it is written, But as many as received Him, to them gave He the right to become children of God, even to them that believe on His name: which were begotten not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And we indeed are begotten of water and of the Spirit, but not thus was Christ begotten of the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For at the time of His Baptism addressing Him, and saying, This is My Son, He did not say, “This has now become My Son,” but, This is My Son; that He might make manifest, that even before the operation of Baptism He was a Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Father begat the Son, not as among men mind begets word.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the mind is substantially existent in us; but the word when spoken is dispersed into the air and comes to an end.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we know Christ to have been begotten not as a word pronounced, but as a Word substantially existing and living; not spoken by the lips, and dispersed, but begotten of the Father eternally and ineffably, in substance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For, In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God, sitting at God’s right hand;—the Word understanding the Father’s will, and creating all things at His bidding: the Word, which came down and went up; for the word of utterance when spoken comes not down, nor goes up; the Word speaking and saying, The things which I have seen with My Father, these I speak: the Word possessed of power, and reigning over all things: for the Father hath committed all things unto the Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Father then begat Him not in such wise as any man could understand, but as Himself only knoweth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we profess not to tell in what manner He begat Him, but we insist that it was not in this manner.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And not we only are ignorant of the generation of the Son from the Father, but so is every created nature.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Speak to the earth, if perchance it may teach thee: and though thou inquire of all things which are upon the earth, they shall not be able to tell thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the earth cannot tell the substance of Him who is its own potter and fashioner.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nor is the earth alone ignorant, but the sun also: for the sun was created on the fourth day, without knowing what had been made in the three days before him; and he who knows not the things made in the three days before him, cannot tell forth the Maker Himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Heaven will not declare this: for at the Father’s bidding the heaven also was like smoke established by Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nor shall the heaven of heavens declare this, nor the waters which are above the heavens.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Why then art thou cast down, O man, at being ignorant of that which even the heavens know not?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay, not only are the heavens ignorant of this generation, but also every angelic nature.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if any one should ascend, were it possible, into the first heaven, and perceiving the ranks of the Angels there should approach and ask them how God begat His own Son, they would say perhaps, “We have above us beings greater and higher; ask them.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Go up to the second heaven and the third; attain, if thou canst, to Thrones, and Dominions, and Principalities, and Powers: and even if any one should reach them, which is impossible, they also would decline the explanation, for they know it not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For my part, I have ever wondered at the curiosity of the bold men, who by their imagined reverence fall into impiety.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though they know nothing of Thrones, and Dominions, and Principalities, and Powers, the workmanship of Christ, they attempt to scrutinise their Creator Himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell me first, O most daring man, wherein does Throne differ from Dominion, and then scrutinise what pertains to Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell me what is a Principality, and what a Power, and what a Virtue, and what an Angel: and then search out their Creator, for all things were made by Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou wilt not, or thou canst not ask Thrones or Dominions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What else is there that knoweth the deep things of God, save only the Holy Ghost, who spake the Divine Scriptures?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But not even the Holy Ghost Himself has spoken in the Scriptures concerning the generation of the Son from the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Why then dost thou busy thyself about things which not even the Holy Ghost has written in the Scriptures?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou that knowest not the things which are written, busiest thou thyself about the things which are not written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There are many questions in the Divine Scriptures; what is written we comprehend not, why do we busy ourselves about what is not written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is sufficient for us to know that God hath begotten One Only Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be not ashamed to confess thine ignorance, since thou sharest ignorance with Angels.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Only He who begat knoweth Him who was begotten, and He who is begotten of Him knoweth Him who begat.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who begat knoweth what He begat: and the Scriptures also testify that He who was begotten is God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For as the Father hath life in Himself, so also hath He given to the Son to have life in Himself; and, that all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father; and, as the Father quickeneth whom He will, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Neither He who begat suffered any loss, nor is anything lacking to Him who was begotten (I know that I have said these things many times, but it is for your safety that they are said so often): neither has He who begat, a Father, nor He who was begotten, a brother.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Neither was He who begat changed into the Son, nor did He who was begotten become the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of One Only Father there is One Only-begotten Son: neither two Unbegotten, nor two Only-begotten; but One Father, Unbegotten (for He is Unbegotten who hath no father); and One Son, eternally begotten of the Father; begotten not in time, but before all ages; not increased by advancement, but begotten that which He now is.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We believe then In the Only-Begotten Son of God, Who Was Begotten of the Father Very God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the True God begetteth not a false god, as we have said, nor did He deliberate and afterwards beget; but He begat eternally, and much more swiftly than our words or thoughts: for we speaking in time, consume time; but in the case of the Divine Power, the generation is timeless.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And as I have often said, He did not bring forth the Son from non-existence into being, nor take the non-existent into sonship: but the Father, being Eternal, eternally and ineffably begat One Only Son, who has no brother.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nor are there two first principles; but the Father is the head of the Son; the beginning is One.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Father begot the Son Very God, called Emmanuel; and Emmanuel being interpreted is, God with us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou know that He who was begotten of the Father, and afterwards became man, is God?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear the Prophet saying, This is our God, none other shall be accounted of in comparison with Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He hath found out every way of knowledge, and given it to Jacob His servant, and to Israel His beloved.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards He was seen on earth, and conversed among men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou herein God become man, after the giving of the law by Moses?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear also a second testimony to Christ’s Deity, that which has just now been read, Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For lest, because of His presence here in the flesh, He should be thought to have been advanced after this to the Godhead, the Scripture says plainly, Therefore God, even Thy God, hath anointed Thee with the oil of gladness above Thy fellows.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou Christ as God anointed by God the Father?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wouldest thou receive yet a third testimony to Christ’s Godhead?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear Esaias saying, Egypt hath laboured, and the merchandise of Ethiopia: and soon after, In Thee shall they make supplication, because God is in Thee, and there is no God save Thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Thou art God, and we knew it not, the God of Israel, the Saviour.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that the Son is God, having in Himself God the Father: saying almost the very same which He has said in the Gospels: The Father is in Me, and I am in the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says not, I am the Father, but the Father is in Me, and I am in the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again He said not, I and the Father am one, but, I and the Father am one, that we should neither separate them, nor make a confusion of Son-Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: One they are because of the dignity pertaining to the Godhead, since God begat God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: One in respect of their kingdom; for the Father reigns not over these, and the Son over those, lifting Himself up against His Father like Absalom: but the kingdom of the Father is likewise the kingdom of the Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: One they are, because there is no discord nor division between them: for what things the Father willeth, the Son willeth the same.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: One, because the creative works of Christ are no other than the Father’s; for the creation of all things is one, the Father having made them through the Son: For He spake, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created, saith the Psalmist.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He who speaks, speaks to one who hears: and He who commands, gives His commandment to one who is present with Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Son then is Very God, having the Father in Himself, not changed into the Father; for the Father was not made man, but the Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For let the truth be freely spoken.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Father suffered not for us, but the Father sent Him who suffered.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Neither let us say, There was a time when the Son was not; nor let us admit a Son who is the Father: but let us walk in the king’s highway; let us turn aside neither on the left hand nor on the right.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Neither from thinking to honour the Son, let us call Him the Father; nor from thinking to honour the Father, imagine the Son to be some one of the creatures.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let One Father be worshipped through One Son, and let not their worship be separated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let One Son be proclaimed, sitting at the right hand of the Father before all ages: sharing His throne not by advancement in time after His Passion, but by eternal possession.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who hath seen the Son, hath seen the Father: for in all things the Son is like to Him who begat Him; begotten Life of Life and Light of Light, Power of Power, God of God; and the characteristics of the Godhead are unchangeable in the Son; and he who is counted worthy to behold Godhead in the Son, attains to the fruition of the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This is not my word, but that of the Only-begotten: Have I been so long time with you, and hast thou not known Me, Philip?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He that hath seen Me, hath seen the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And to be brief, let us neither separate them, nor make a confusion: neither say thou ever that the Son is foreign to the Father, nor admit those who say that the Father is at one time Father, and at another Son: for these are strange and impious statements, and not the doctrines of the Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Father having begotten the Son, remained the Father and is not changed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He begat Wisdom, yet lost not wisdom Himself; and begat Power, yet became not weak: He begat God, but lost not His own Godhead: and neither did He lose anything Himself by diminution or change; nor has He who was begotten any thing wanting.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Perfect is He who begat, Perfect that which was begotten: God was He who begat, God He who was begotten; God of all Himself, yet entitling the Father His own God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He is not ashamed to say, I ascend unto My Father and your Father, and to My God and your God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But lest thou shouldest think that He is in a like sense Father of the Son and of the creatures, Christ drew a distinction in what follows.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He said not, “I ascend to our Father,” lest the creatures should be made fellows of the Only-begotten; but He said, My Father and your Father; in one way Mine, by nature; in another yours, by adoption.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, to my God and your God, in one way Mine, as His true and Only-begotten Son, and in another way yours, as His workmanship.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Son of God then is Very God, ineffably begotten before all ages (for I say the same things often to you, that it may be graven upon your mind).

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This also believe, that God has a Son: but about the manner be not curious, for by searching thou wilt not find.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Exalt not thyself, lest thou fall: think upon those things only which have been commanded thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell me first what He is who begat, and then learn that which He begat; but if thou canst not conceive the nature of Him who hath begotten, search not curiously into the manner of that which is begotten.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For godliness it sufficeth thee to know, as we have said, that God hath One Only Son, One naturally begotten; who began not His being when He was born in Bethlehem, but Before All Ages.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For hear the Prophet Micah saying, And thou, Bethlehem, house of Ephrata, art little to be among the thousands of Judah.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Out of thee shall come forth unto Me a Ruler, who shall feed My people Israel: and His goings forth are from the beginning, from days of eternity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Think not then of Him who is now come forth out of Bethlehem, but worship Him who was eternally begotten of the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Suffer none to speak of a beginning of the Son in time, but as a timeless Beginning acknowledge the Father.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Father is the Beginning of the Son, timeless, incomprehensible, without beginning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The fountain of the river of righteousness, even of the Only-begotten, is the Father, who begat Him as Himself only knoweth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou know that our Lord Jesus Christ is King Eternal?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear Him again saying, Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day, and he saw it, and was glad.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And then, when the Jews received this hardly, He says what to them was still harder, Before Abraham was, I am.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again He saith to the Father, And now, Father, glorify Thou Me with Thine own self, with the glory which I had with Thee before the world was.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says plainly, “before the world was, I had the glory which is with Thee.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again when He says, For Thou lovedst Me before the foundation of the world, He plainly declares, “The glory which I have with thee is from eternity.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We believe then In One Lord Jesus Christ, the Only-Begotten Son of God, Begotten of His Father Very God Before All Worlds, by Whom All Things Were Made.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers, all things were made through Him, and of things created none is exempted from His authority.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Silenced be every heresy which brings in different creators and makers of the world; silenced the tongue which blasphemes the Christ the Son of God; let them be silenced who say that the sun is the Christ, for He is the sun’s Creator, not the sun which we see.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Silenced be they who say that the world is the workmanship of Angels, who wish to steal away the dignity of the Only-begotten.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For whether visible or invisible, whether thrones or dominions, or anything that is named, all things were made by Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He reigns over the things which have been made by Him, not having seized another’s spoils, but reigning over His own workmanship, even as the Evangelist John has said, All things were made by Him, and without Him was not anything made.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: All things were made by Him, the Father working by the Son.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I wish to give also a certain illustration of what I am saying, but I know that it is feeble; for of things visible what can be an exact illustration of the Divine Power?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But nevertheless as feeble be it spoken by the feeble to the feeble.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For just as any king, whose son was a king, if he wished to form a city, might suggest to his son, his partner in the kingdom, the form of the city, and he having received the pattern, brings the design to completion; so, when the Father wished to form all things, the Son created all things at the Father’s bidding, that the act of bidding might secure to the Father His absolute authority, and yet the Son in turn might have authority over His own workmanship, and neither the Father be separated from the lordship over His own works, nor the Son rule over things created by others, but by Himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For, as I have said, Angels did not create the world, but the Only-begotten Son, begotten, as I have said, before all ages, By Whom All Things Were Made, nothing having been excepted from His creation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And let this suffice to have been spoken by us so far, by the grace of Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But let us now recur to our profession of the Faith, and so for the present finish our discourse.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Christ made all things, whether thou speak of Angels, or Archangels, of Dominions, or Thrones.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not that the Father wanted strength to create the works Himself, but because He willed that the Son should reign over His own workmanship, God Himself giving Him the design of the things to be made.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For honouring His own Father the Only-begotten saith, The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do; for what things soever He doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work, there being no opposition in those who work.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For all Mine are Thine, and Thine are Mine, saith the Lord in the Gospels.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this we may certainly know from the Old and New Testaments.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He who said, Let us make man in our image and after our likeness, was certainly speaking to some one present.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But clearest of all are the Psalmist’s words, He spake and they were made; He commanded, and they were created, as if the Father commanded and spake, and the Son made all things at the Father’s bidding.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this Job said mystically, Which alone spread out the heaven, and walketh upon the sea as on firm ground; signifying to those who understand that He who when present here walked upon the sea is also He who aforetime made the heavens.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again the Lord saith, Or didst Thou take earth, and fashion clay into a living being? then afterwards, Are the gates of death opened to Thee through fear, and did the door-keepers of hell shudder at sight of Thee? thus signifying that He who through loving-kindness descended into hell, also in the beginning made man out of clay.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Christ then is the Only-begotten Son of God, and Maker of the world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He was in the world, and the world was made by Him; and He came unto His own, as the Gospel teaches us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And not only of the things which are seen, but also of the things which are not seen, is Christ the Maker at the Father’s bidding.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in Him, according to the Apostle, were all things created that are in the heavens, and that are upon the earth, things visible and invisible, whether thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers; all things have been created by Him and for Him; and He is before all, and in Him all things consist.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even if thou speak of the worlds, of these also Jesus Christ is the Maker by the Father’s bidding.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in these last days God spake unto us by His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, by whom also He made the worlds.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To whom be the glory, honour, might, now and ever, and world without end.
